Most traders spend years learning strategies, setups and indicators without ever realizing that almost every approach to trading is built on the same two concepts. Market structure and time frame alignment. Without these two working together, it does not matter what strategy you use, what key levels you mark, or how good your entries look. The edge will not be there.
In this video we break down both concepts from the ground up. You will learn the difference between a break of structure and a market structure shift, what continuation and reversal actually look like on a real chart, and why most traders misread the market because they are only looking at one time frame.
We also go into time frame alignment and top down analysis, how to use two or three time frames depending on your trading style, and how a higher time frame key level is what actually validates a structure on the lower time frame. This is the missing piece that turns an average setup into a high probability trade.
By the end of the video you will have a full framework to build your own trading plan using any key level you already use, whether that is imbalances, order blocks, supply and demand, engulfing candles or support and resistance.
If you trade forex, gold, indices or any other market and you want to finally understand why your strategy is inconsistent, this video will give you the answer. Enjoy.
